title: gilia gracilis var. micrantha
intro: Gilia gracilis var. micrantha, also known as the smallflower gilia, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the honeysuckle family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Gilia gracilis var. micrantha can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
